claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05); Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for? If yes, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have a child I'm asking about - my son Alex. He's 8 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions:

First, do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Now, does Alex live with you in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to understand your situation better.

Do you make any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep, such as paying for food, clothes, pocket money, or helping with accommodation costs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do help out when I can. I contribute towards things like clothes and other bits he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To determine your eligibility, I need to understand the amount you're contributing.

Do you contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment (or the equivalent value) towards Alex's upkeep? For reference, Child Benefit is currently £25.60 per week for the eldest/only child, or about £110 per month. This can include contributions for food, clothes, pocket money, or helping to provide him with somewhere to live.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't contribute that much. I give about £10 a week towards his things - it's what I can manage at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for being honest about your situation. I have one more important question:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, such as the person he lives with?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, the person he lives with already claims Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

1. **Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child** - Someone else (the person Alex lives with) is already claiming Child Benefit for him. Two people cannot receive Child Benefit for the same child.

2. **Contribution requirement not met** - When a child doesn't live with you, you would need to contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment (£25.60 per week or approximately £110 per month) towards Alex's upkeep to qualify. Your current contribution of £10 per week, while valuable, falls below this threshold.

I understand this may be disappointing, especially as you're doing what you can to support Alex. The person who Alex lives with and who is currently claiming Child Benefit is the eligible claimant in this situation.

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if Alex comes to live with you, or if you're able to increase your contributions and the other person stops claiming - you could potentially become eligible. In that case, you would need to make a new claim.

Is there anything else I can help clarify about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, for Child Benefit eligibility when a child doesn't live with you, you must be contributing at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment towards the child's upkeep. The Child Benefit rate is £25.60 per week (approximately £110 per month) for an eldest or only child. The claimant is contributing £10 per week, which is less than the required amount. Additionally, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child, and someone else (the person Alex lives with) is already claiming Child Benefit for him. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 8), who does not live with them. While the claimant makes financial contributions of £10 per week towards Alex's upkeep, this falls short of the minimum requirement of £25.60 per week (the Child Benefit rate) needed to qualify when a child lives elsewhere. Furthermore, another person is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child. The claimant is therefore not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, for Child Benefit eligibility when a child doesn't live with you, you must be contributing at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment towards the child's upkeep. The Child Benefit rate is £25.60 per week (approximately £110 per month) for an eldest or only child. The claimant is contributing £10 per week, which is less than the required amount. Additionally, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child, and someone else (the person Alex lives with) is already claiming Child Benefit for him. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 8), who does not live with them. While the claimant makes financial contributions of £10 per week towards Alex's upkeep, this falls short of the minimum requirement of £25.60 per week (the Child Benefit rate) needed to qualify when a child lives elsewhere. Furthermore, another person is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child. The claimant is therefore not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
